facility_name: Tesla Cortex AI Supercluster -- Giga Texas (130MW+, Liquid-Cooled)
full_address: Gigafactory Texas, 13101 Harold Green Road, Austin, TX
build_phase_if_known: Cortex 1 operational at full capacity (June 2025). Cortex 2 under construction (200MW).
cooling_type: Direct liquid cooling (Supermicro DLC), cooling towers with reclaimed rainwater storage tanks
opportunity_summary: Tesla Cortex is a 130MW liquid-cooled AI supercluster at Giga Texas, running 100,000+ Nvidia H100/H200 GPUs to train FSD (Full Self-Driving) and Optimus robotics neural networks. Supermicro provides the DLC technology. Cooling towers use reclaimed rainwater stored in 4 underground tanks. Elon Musk confirmed full-capacity operation in June 2025. Cortex 2 (200MW) is under construction at the same campus. Total planned capacity exceeds 500MW.
confidence_reason: Musk confirmed full-capacity operation June 2025. Supermicro CEO referenced liquid-cooled Tesla data center. Multiple sources confirm 130MW initial, 500MW+ planned. Tom's Hardware, Teslarati, DCD, C2O Group case study all corroborate.
source_1_title: Tesla FSD Cortex AI Supercluster Running at Full Capacity, Confirms Elon Musk
source_1_evidence_snippet: Elon Musk confirms Tesla Cortex supercluster at Giga Texas running at full capacity. Cooling tower fans visible in aerial footage.
source_2_title: Elon Musk's Liquid-Cooled 'Gigafactory' AI Data Centers Get a Plug from Supermicro CEO
source_2_evidence_snippet: Tesla and xAI's new supercomputers will have 350,000 Nvidia GPUs. Supermicro CEO confirms liquid-cooled gigafactory data centers.
notes: AUTOMOTIVE SECTOR. Tesla Cortex trains FSD autonomous driving and Optimus humanoid robot AI. Uses Supermicro DLC (claims 89% cooling energy reduction vs air). Cortex 2 under construction for 200MW. Total campus planned >500MW. Half Tesla custom silicon, half Nvidia. Reclaimed rainwater cooling. C2O Americas handles telecom and MEP commissioning.
current_phase: Cortex 1 at full capacity; Cortex 2 under construction
expansion_details: Cortex 2 under construction (200MW). Total campus planned to exceed 500MW within 18 months of June 2024 announcement.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Elon Musk reveals power needs: 130MW of power and cooling this year, increasing to >500MW over next 18 months.

facility_name: Tesla Dojo Supercomputer -- Palo Alto (Water-Cooled D1 ExaPods, Shut Down Aug 2025 / Restarted Jan 2026)
customer_type_if_known: Tesla internal (autonomous driving neural net training)
build_phase_if_known: 7 ExaPods planned in Palo Alto. Shut down Aug 2025, restarted Jan 2026 with new chip iteration.
cooling_type: Water-cooled Training Tiles with cold-plate bare-die contact; custom cooling distribution unit (CDU) per ExaPod
opportunity_summary: Tesla's Dojo is a custom AI supercomputer using proprietary D1 chips (25 per Training Tile, 3,000 per ExaPod). Each water-cooled Training Tile consumes 15kW with bare-die cold-plate contact and secondary VRM cooling. Power density exceeds 200kW per cabinet. Tripped a San Jose substation during 2.3MW load test in 2022. Planned 7 ExaPods in Palo Alto. Powered on for production August 2023. Shut down August 2025 ('evolutionary dead end' -- Musk), restarted January 2026 with new chip iteration.
confidence_reason: Wikipedia, TechCrunch, DCD, Electrek all confirm water-cooled Training Tiles, D1 chip specs, August 2025 shutdown and January 2026 restart. 200kW+ per cabinet confirmed.
source_1_evidence_snippet: Water-cooled Training Tile packages 25 D1 chips into 5x5 array. ExaPOD: 3,000 D1 chips, 1 exaflop BF16. August 2025 disbanded, restarted January 2026.
source_2_title: Tesla Dojo: The Rise and Fall of Elon Musk's AI Supercomputer
source_2_evidence_snippet: Tesla disbanded its Dojo team and shut down the project in mid-August 2025. Lead Peter Bannon left. Restarted in January 2026.
notes: AUTOMOTIVE SECTOR. Custom D1 chip (7nm TSMC), 354 training FLOPs per chip. Each Training Tile: 15kW, 288A at 52V. Dual cold-plate design (bare-die + VRM). Planned for FSD neural network training. ~20 Dojo team members left to found DensityAI after shutdown. Exact Palo Alto building address not publicly disclosed.
current_phase: Restarted Jan 2026 with new chip iteration after Aug 2025 shutdown
expansion_details: Original plan for 7 ExaPods was abandoned. Cortex at Giga Texas is the primary AI compute facility now.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Powered on for production Aug 2023. Shut down Aug 2025. Restarted Jan 2026 with new chip iteration.

facility_name: Shell HPC Data Center -- Skybox Houston One, Katy TX (1.2MW Immersion-Cooled)
build_phase_if_known: Operational, 864 servers deployed in GRC ICEraQ immersion pods.
cooling_type: Single-phase immersion cooling (GRC ICEraQ Series 10 Duo); Shell Immersion Cooling Fluid (ElectroSafe)
opportunity_summary: Shell deployed 864 dual-socket servers (AMD EPYC 9654, 96-core, 4th Gen) in 6 GRC ICEraQ Series 10 Duo immersion cooling pods at Skybox Houston One in Katy, Texas. Total: 1,728 processors, 165,888 cores, 1.2MW compute. Servers fully submerged in Shell's own ElectroSafe immersion cooling fluid. Each GRC pod cools 200kW using warm facility water (up to 100kW per 42U rack with 90F water). Net-zero operational emissions claimed via Shell Energy North America 100% renewable power. Penguin Solutions provided the HPC cluster.
confidence_reason: GRC customer success page, AMD case study, DCD, Penguin Solutions blog, and Shell Energy case study all confirm 864 servers, GRC ICEraQ, AMD EPYC 9654, 1.2MW, Shell ElectroSafe fluid. Extremely well-documented.
source_1_title: Shell's Skybox Houston One Data Center
source_1_evidence_snippet: Shell deployed GRC ICEraQ Series 10 Duo at Skybox Houston One, Katy TX. 1.2MW compute, 200kW per pod using warm facility water. Energy sector.
source_2_title: Boosting HPC Capacity with Net-Zero Operational Emissions at Shell's Houston Data Center
source_2_evidence_snippet: 864 dual-socket servers, AMD EPYC 9654 96-core CPUs, 1,728 processors, 165,888 cores. Immersion-cooled with GRC pods.
notes: ENERGY SECTOR. Shell is both an immersion cooling fluid vendor (Shell Immersion Cooling Fluid S5 X / ElectroSafe) and an end-user deploying its own fluid in its own HPC facility. Intel certified Shell's fluids first among major chip manufacturers. Shell also launched a DLC fluid in June 2025 and adapted immersion tech for battery storage (BESS) with QingAn Energy Storage in Oct 2025. Shell is a GRC ElectroSafe Fluid Partner.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Shell IT: 1.2MW compute in GRC ICEraQ immersion pods at Skybox Houston One, Katy TX. 864 servers, AMD EPYC 9654.
workload_type: Oil & gas HPC (seismic, reservoir simulation, exploration)

facility_name: Volkswagen Group HPC DC -- Green Mountain DC2-Telemark, Rjukan, Norway (2.75MW)
customer_type_if_known: Volkswagen Passenger Cars and Audi (internal HPC)
cooling_type: Indirect free cooling leveraging cold Norwegian climate; cold water temperature adjustments
opportunity_summary: Volkswagen Group operates 2.75MW of HPC capacity in 2 dedicated data halls at Green Mountain's DC2-Telemark facility in Rjukan, Norway. Used by VW Passenger Cars and Audi for crash test simulations and virtual wind tunnel tests. Climate-neutral operation using 100% Norwegian hydroelectric power. Saves ~5,800 tons CO2/year vs fossil-powered DC. Built in 6 months using Schneider Electric prefabricated modular infrastructure. Indirect free cooling exploits Norway's cold/wet climate. Green Mountain PUE typically 1.10-1.20. NOTE: This is free-air/water cooling, NOT liquid-cooled server hardware -- included as automotive sector DC with advanced cooling.
confidence_reason: DCD, Green Mountain official page, Volkswagen Group press release all confirm 2.75MW, 2 halls, Rjukan location, hydroelectric power, crash test / wind tunnel HPC workloads. April 2019 opening confirmed.
source_1_title: Volkswagen Group Officially Opens Data Centre at Green Mountain's Colocation Site in Norway
source_1_evidence_snippet: Volkswagen Group opens 2.75MW data centre at Green Mountain TEL-Rjukan. 100% renewable hydroelectric power. HPC for vehicle development.
source_2_title: Green Computing Performance: Volkswagen Opens Carbon Neutral Data Center in Norway
source_2_evidence_snippet: Volkswagen opens carbon-neutral data center in Rjukan, Norway. Saves 5,800 tons CO2/year. Hydroelectric power.
notes: AUTOMOTIVE SECTOR. VW also has HPC at Verne Global Iceland (~1MW since 2016) for similar workloads. VW AG aims to make all data centers climate-neutral by 2027. Rjukan site uses Schneider Electric prefab modules. Indirect free cooling, not liquid-cooled servers -- but represents automotive sector HPC presence. VW Group includes VW, Audi, Porsche, Lamborghini, SEAT, Skoda.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: VW Group: 2.75MW HPC in 2 data halls at Green Mountain DC2-Telemark, Rjukan Norway. Crash test and wind tunnel simulations.
workload_type: Automotive HPC (crash simulation, virtual wind tunnel, vehicle development)

facility_name: BMW D3 Platform Data Center -- Unterschleissheim, Munich (Autonomous Driving HPC)
customer_type_if_known: BMW Group internal (autonomous driving, Data-Driven Development)
opportunity_summary: BMW Group's D3 (Data-Driven Development) platform data center is sited near the Autonomous Driving Campus in Unterschleissheim, Munich. Houses 230+ petabytes of storage, 100,000+ cores, and 200+ GPUs. Collects 1,500+ TB raw driving data daily, pushes 50PB to Hardware-in-the-Loop stations every 2 weeks via 96x100Gbps connection (3.75 Tbit/s). Operated by DXC Technology. Cooling type not publicly disclosed -- but at this scale and density, liquid cooling is highly probable. NOTE: Cooling technology not confirmed; included as major automotive sector AI/HPC DC.

facility_name: Stellium DC / Submer / ExxonMobil OCP Immersion Cooling Deployment -- Newcastle, UK
build_phase_if_known: OCP ORv3 proof of concept completed Jan 2025. First ORv3 showcase in Europe.
cooling_type: Single-phase immersion cooling (Submer pods); ExxonMobil DC 3235 Super dielectric coolant
opportunity_summary: Stellium Data Centres deployed Submer immersion cooling technology at their Newcastle campus using ExxonMobil's Submer-validated DC 3235 Super coolant. Compute powered by MiTAC Capri 2 servers with AMD CPUs (Circle B supplied). This is the first OCP ORv3 (Open Rack v3) showcase deployed in Europe and reportedly the largest OCP stack in Europe. Proof of concept completed January 2025. Open day held February 12, 2025 for customers and OCP representatives. MW capacity not publicly disclosed.
confidence_reason: Submer press release, Stellium press release, DCD, Data Centre Solutions, and Capacity Media all confirm Newcastle location, Submer immersion, ExxonMobil DC 3235 Super fluid, MiTAC Capri 2 / AMD servers, OCP ORv3. MW not disclosed.

source_1_evidence_snippet: Immersion cooling OCP PoC completed at Stellium UK HPC hyperscale facility. First ORv3 showcase in Europe.
source_2_title: Stellium, Submer and Circle B Unite for OCP Liquid Cooling Technology Deployment in Newcastle
source_2_evidence_snippet: Submer immersion cooling deployed at Stellium Newcastle with ExxonMobil DC 3235 Super coolant. MiTAC Capri 2 servers, AMD CPUs, Circle B components.
notes: ENERGY SECTOR INVOLVEMENT (ExxonMobil as coolant supplier). ExxonMobil provides Submer-validated DC 3235 Super coolant. ExxonMobil is a confirmed Submer customer (per TechCrunch funding article). ExxonMobil's full immersion fluid portfolio includes DC 3152/3150/315, 3220, 3235 Super, 3250, 1150, 1210. Hydrocarbon-based coolants outperform fluorocarbon in high-flow scenarios per ExxonMobil models. ExxonMobil also partnered with Intel on immersion cooling validation.

facility_name: DUG Technology HPC DC -- Skybox Houston, TX (15MW Immersion-Cooled, Oil & Gas)
full_address: Skybox Datacenters, Houston Energy Corridor, TX
build_phase_if_known: Operational. 15MW leased at Skybox. 40,000 servers in DUG Cool immersion.
cooling_type: DUG Cool proprietary immersion cooling (servers fully submerged in dielectric fluid); 720+ enclosures
opportunity_summary: DUG Technology, Australia's leading geoscience HPC provider, leased 15MW at Skybox Datacenters in Houston's Energy Corridor. 40,000 servers fully immersed in DUG Cool dielectric fluid across 720+ enclosures, delivering 250 petaflops. DUG Cool is a patented immersion system refined over a decade, reducing power consumption by up to 51% vs air cooling by eliminating refrigerated cooling and fans. Serves global oil & gas industry for seismic data processing. Also the largest commercial user of Intel Xeon Phi co-processors (historically). Previously used GRC CarnotJet System at Perth facility.
confidence_reason: Data Center Frontier, DCD, DUG official site, Data Center Knowledge all confirm 15MW at Skybox Houston, 40,000 servers, DUG Cool immersion, 720+ enclosures, 250 petaflops. Well-documented.

DUG Cool available as containerized 1MW units (10ft, 20ft, 40ft).
